title: Boat Rental in Tromsoe: 2026 Guide to Costs, Seasons, and Tips
html:
Boat hire Tromsoe opens up the stunning fjords of northern Norway, with departures from Prostneset harbour. Expect to pay from EUR 250 for a half-day motorboat rental, while a crewed yacht for a full day can reach EUR 1,200. BoatHire24 connects you with verified local providers.

Where to set off in Tromsoe

Prostneset harbour, at the city centre, is the main departure point for rental boats. Other real marinas include Tromsoe Marina, Skattora, and the small quay at Kvaloya. Many trips head towards Sandnessundet or the nearby island of Kvaloya. Tromsoe Airport is a short drive from most harbours.

You can also launch from the village of Sommaroy, 50 km west, known for its white sand beaches. Tromsoysundet is a calm sheltered channel ideal for beginners.

Which boat to rent

  1. Small motorboat (20-25 ft) best for pairs exploring Tromsoysundet; sleeps none; from EUR 250 half-day.
  2. Sports cruiser (e.g. Axopar 28) for groups of 4-6 visiting Sandnessundet; from EUR 500 half-day.
  3. Motor yacht (e.g. Princess V58) for 8 guests on a full-day fjord cruise; from EUR 1,500 per day. See the Princess V58 listing for details and availability.
  4. Rigid inflatable boat (RIB) for 6-8 people, perfect for whale watching in Skjervoy; from EUR 350 half-day.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Tromsoe?

Prices range from EUR 100 per person for a shared tour to EUR 2,000 per day for a crewed motor yacht.

  • Shared tour (2-3 hours): EUR 80-120 per person, common in October to March.
  • Small motorboat half-day (4 hours): from EUR 250.
  • Catamaran full-day (up to 12 guests): from EUR 900.
  • Crewed yacht full-day (up to 8 guests): from EUR 1,200.

For accurate rates on boat hire Tromsoe, compare listings on BoatHire24 as prices vary by provider and season.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Tromsoe?

In Norway, no licence is required for boats under 8 metres or 25 hp, but operators may still impose conditions.

Always confirm with the provider before booking. Many rental companies in Tromsoe offer a brief on-water instruction. For larger vessels like the Princess V58, a skippered charter is typically required.

Local tip: in high season (June to August), the midnight sun means you can boat until 11 pm. Be mindful of weather changes, as the wind can pick up quickly in Tromsoysundet.

Season for boat rental in Tromsoe

High season runs from June to August, with warmest air temperatures around 15°C. The midnight sun allows extended cruising. Winter (October to March) is prime for whale watching, but you need a heated cabin boat. The prevailing wind is the south-westerly, but local fjords offer shelter.

At a glance

  • Price band: EUR 80 per person to EUR 2,000 per day
  • Peak months: June, July, August
  • Trip length: half-day (4 h) to full day (8 h)
  • Typical capacity: 2 to 12 guests
  • Currency: EUR

Questions about boat rental in Tromsoe

Can I rent a boat without a licence in Tromsoe?

Yes, for smaller boats under 8 metres or low horsepower, operators often waive the licence requirement after a safety briefing.

What is the best boat for whale watching near Tromsoe?

A rigid inflatable boat (RIB) offers speed and stability, while a small motorboat with a heated cabin provides comfort in winter.

How far can I travel from Tromsoe in a half-day rental?

A half-day allows a round trip of 20-30 nautical miles, e.g. from Prostneset to Kvaloya and back.

Closing

Whether you want to explore the fjords under the midnight sun or watch whales in winter, renting a boat in Tromsoe is straightforward with BoatHire24. If you own a boat in the region, you can also list it on BoatHire24 to reach travellers worldwide.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Tromsoe and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
